rulings.Case Title: J K SELZNICK VS. ANDREW M ZACKS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Andrew Zack'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301 Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 7, Defendant Andrew Zack's Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED IN PART, ON THE CONDUCT OR ADVISE OF MCCLOSKEY, CENTRAL TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS THE CONDUCT OF COUNSEL AND THEIR CLIENT IN THE FIRST AND SECOND CASES, IN PARTICULAR, THAT THE JUDGMENT WAS OBTAINED BY "EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR MISTAKE" (AMENDED COMPLAINT PARAGRAPHS 9, 14 AND 15). PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF HER CONTENTION THAT SHE WAS NOT SERVED. SHE RELIES ONLY ON THE MCCLOSKEY DECLARATION THAT PLAINTIFF FOLLOWED COUNSEL'S ADVISE. THAT IS NOT SUFFICIENT. =(302/PJM/AL)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MITCHELL ENGINEERING, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 9, DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE ISSUE RAISED RE LIQUIDATED DAMAGES IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DEMURRER. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DAMAGES, GENERAL, COMPENSATORY AND CONSEQUENTIAL. AS SUCH A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS ALLEGED. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MITCHELL ENGINEERING, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 10, DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. A CONTRACT IS PLED IN PARAGRAPHS 5-9. A BREACH IS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPHS 15, 16, 22, AND 23, PLAINTIFF'S PERFORMANCE IS ALLEGED, AND DIFFERENT FORMS OF DAMAGES ARE ALLEGED. THE ACCEPTANCE ARGUMENT CANNOT BE RAISED BY DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: THE CADLE COMPANY VS. KIM MICHAEL GALE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 16, DEFENDANT KIM GALE'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRODUCE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT THE JUDGMENT IS ENFORCEABLE. THE DECLARATION OF KNOLES FAILS TO SET FORTH AN ADEQUATE FOUNDATION FOR HIS BASIS TO AUTHENTICATE EXHIBITS A - E. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT HAS RAISED A MATERIAL DISPUTE OF FACT AS TO EXHIBIT B AND D HAS FAILED TO MEET THE BEST EVIDENCE REQUIREMENT OF EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1520, IN PARTICULAR PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO OBTAIN A CERTIFIED COPY OF THE DOCUMENT FROM THE TEXAS COURT. BECAUSE B IS NOT ADMISSIBLE, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF HAS AN ENFORCEABLE JUDGMENT. =(302/PJM/MW-X)
